## Key Technical Findings (Extracted from Transcripts)

### 1. Weber vs Gauss - His Core Philosophy

**Source:** "What is Gauss? What why I don't care!" (2024-03-15) + "How to really compare every mat" (2025-05-17) + "Uncovering the Secrets of the Gauss Revolution" (2023-02-24)

**His position: Gauss alone is meaningless. Weber is what matters for therapeutic PEMF.**

Key quotes from transcripts:
- "If I tell you that my coil makes 50 gauss, 100 gauss, 1,000 gauss, what I'm describing is ONE line and how much pressure there is on that line."
- "Weber on the other hand is how many of those flux lines are in a specific area."
- "When your coils are flat on the mat and you got 10 coils making 10 gauss each, you don't have 100 gauss. You still only have 10 gauss. But you're going to have a lot more Weber."

**Water analogy (from transcript):**
- Gauss = PSI (pressure of water) like a pressure washer: 1,000-7,000 PSI, 1-10 gallons/minute
- Weber = Volume of water like a flooding river: very low PSI but insane volume
- "The river is not going to cut you, but it's going to push you around because of the volume."
- A pressure washer (high gauss) can cut through steel but only in a tiny area. A river (high weber) moves everything in its path.

**From pemfmatsource.com:** "Gauss (G) only measures the intensity of a single magnetic flux line rather than the quality of the entire magnetic field. True performance depends on coil material, quantity, and placement -- not Gauss ratings alone."

### 2. Recommended Specifications

**Source:** Multiple transcripts cross-referenced

| Parameter | Steeve's Recommendation | Reasoning |
|-----------|------------------------|-----------|
| **Gauss range** | 30-100 gauss per coil (sweet spot ~45-55 gauss) | Above 100 gauss caused chest tightness; below 30 is too weak |
| **Frequency** | 1-100 Hz (1-300 Hz is effective range) | No "magic frequency" exists; different Hz for different purposes |
| **Day frequency** | 14 Hz (unipolar) | Keeps you awake and alert |
| **Afternoon frequency** | 8 Hz | Relaxation, mellow |
| **Sleep frequency** | 1 Hz | Deep sleep induction |
| **Waveform** | Square wave (non-negotiable) | NASA study proved square wave is king. Delta wave ~70-80% as effective. Sine/triangle/sawtooth = no significant effect |
| **Signal type** | DC square wave (unipolar or bipolar) | Bipolar mimics NASA 10Hz study. No research proving one better than other |
| **Coil wire** | 22-gauge magnet wire (enamel coated) | Math is optimized for this gauge with 19.5V power supply |
| **Coil wraps** | 95 wraps per coil | Calculated for optimal field with power supply specs |
| **Coil form** | Propane bottle diameter (~100mm/4in) | Creates optimal EMF field geometry |
| **Coil resistance** | ~3.3 ohms per coil | Two coils in series = 6.6 ohms |
| **Power supply** | 19.5V DC (laptop-style) | 24V max, 6 amps max = 150 watts. Safe, cannot burn house down |
| **Voltage** | 24V maximum to skin | Skin protects against up to 60V DC |
| **Controller** | ZK-PP2K (basic) or Nextion (advanced) | PWM signal generators, square wave output |
| **Duty cycle** | 20-50% (32% commonly used) | Does NOT improve healing efficiency; only generates heat on mat. Higher duty = more heat (heating blanket effect) |
| **Coils per mat (full)** | 10-20 coils | ZK line: 15-coil or 20-coil full body |
| **Coils per mat (mini)** | 3-6 coils | Three coils per series, two series in parallel |
| **Slew rate** | As fast as possible | Critical for energy transfer at cellular level. Square wave has fastest slew rate |

### 3. Why Square Wave is King

**Source:** "Square Wave Wins Again!" (2022-10-28) + "Wave form, NOT What you think" (2024-04-03) + "high power vs low Power PEMF who is right?" (2024-01-19)

Steeve cites two key studies:
1. **NASA 2003 study** - Tested square wave, sinusoidal, triangular, sawtooth, delta pulse, and static magnetic field on muscle cell growth in petri dishes
2. **University of Michigan replication** - Independently confirmed NASA results

Results from his transcript summary:
- **Square wave:** Consistently increased cell colony density (WINNER)
- **Delta pulse wave:** ~70-80% as effective as square wave
- **Triangular wave:** Inconsistent results, could not be reliably repeated
- **Sinusoidal wave:** No visual effect on cell colony formation
- **Sawtooth wave:** No significant effect
- **Static magnetic field (magnets):** No effect, even at 1,000-2,500 gauss
- **Control (no treatment):** No growth

**His explanation of why square wave works:** The slew rate (how fast the signal rises from 0 to peak) is what creates cellular energy transfer through induction. Square wave has the fastest possible slew rate (essentially vertical rise). When the magnetic field CHANGES, energy transfers. When it's static (even if strong), nothing happens. This is why permanent magnets don't work for PEMF.

### 4. Constructive vs Destructive Fields

**Source:** "EMF Field and Duty Cycle" (2024-05-24) + "high power vs low Power" (2024-01-19) + "PEMF company do this wrong" (2024-09-06)

**Critical finding: Coil orientation determines whether you get healing or dead spots.**

- **Constructive field:** All coils pointing same direction (north up). Creates uniform "bubble" of EMF coverage. Much larger Weber area.
- **Destructive field:** One or more coils reversed. Creates dead spots where fields cancel each other out. Dramatically reduced effective coverage.

"Many PEMF companies don't even care about that. So you get sometimes a destructive field, sometimes a constructive field. You never know what you're getting."

**How to check:** Use a compass on each coil. When powered on, the compass needle should deflect the same direction on every coil. If one deflects opposite, that coil needs to be flipped.

### 5. Duty Cycle - Not What You Think

**Source:** "EMF Field and Duty Cycle Explained" (2024-05-24)

**Key insight: Duty cycle does NOT affect healing effectiveness.**

Using a compass analogy:
- The compass bezel represents a cell in your body
- The needle touching the bottom of the bezel = energy transfer moment
- Energy transfers at the INSTANT the magnetic field changes (needle touches down)
- It does NOT matter how long the needle stays down or up
- A 10% duty cycle and 50% duty cycle transfer the same energy per pulse

"The duty cycle does not improve or reduce the efficiencies of the PMF, the healing part. All it does is create heat on the mat. So if you're in the winter, you want to feel some heat on the mat, be more like a little bit of a heating blanket, you can bring the duty cycle up to 20."

### 6. Why High-Gauss PEMF is Overhyped

**Source:** "high power vs low Power PEMF who is right?" (2024-01-19) + "If You Can't Explain It Simply" (2025-02-15)

Steeve's argument against high-gauss devices:

1. **More gauss does NOT penetrate deeper in a useful way.** High gauss is like a pressure washer - extremely focused but tiny area. What you need for whole-body therapy is COVERAGE (Weber), not pressure (gauss).

2. **He built a 180 gauss/coil mat and it caused adverse effects.** "It gives me kind of a chest anxiety attack almost, and I'm not the only one that said that." Multiple users of his high-gauss test mat reported chest tightness.

3. **Sweet spot is 30-100 gauss.** His preferred range is 45-55 gauss per coil. Below 30 is too weak to be effective. Above 100 starts causing uncomfortable effects.

4. **Companies use gauss as marketing.** "If I have 10 coils making 10 gauss each, that doesn't equal 100 gauss. Not the way they place. You still only have 10 gauss but with more Weber." Companies inflate gauss numbers by adding coil counts or misrepresenting measurements.

5. **Static high-gauss magnets (1,000-2,500 gauss) showed ZERO effect** in both NASA and University of Michigan studies. The pulsing (changing field) is what matters, not the strength.

### 7. How PEMF Actually Works (His Explanation)

**Source:** "Everything I know about PEMF in less than 20 mins" (2024-04-25) + "high power vs low Power" (2024-01-19)

Steeve's framework:
1. **PEMF does NOT directly heal you.** It helps your body heal itself.
2. **Primary mechanism:** PEMF reduces/modulates chronic inflammation, which is the root cause of ~80% of human health issues.
3. **Secondary mechanism:** Improves cellular signaling - cells communicate better, function properly.
4. **Cellular dysfunction** (cells not working properly) causes: premature aging, cancer development, increased cell death.
5. **Chronic inflammation** causes: heart disease, brain fog, eczema, memory problems, joint pain, arthritis, weakened immune system.
6. **PEMF rebalances your immune system** first, which signals inflammation reduction, which opens up blood flow, which allows nutrients and oxygen to reach cells, which enables natural healing.
7. **The body is like a city:** roads = circulatory system, power grid = nervous system, sewage = waste removal. PEMF improves the "infrastructure" so the "city" runs better.

### 8. Competitor/Industry Critique

**Source:** "If You Can't Explain It Simply" (2025-02-15) + "How to really compare every mat" (2025-05-17) + "PEMF company do this wrong" (2024-09-06)

**What PEMF companies do wrong (per Steeve):**

1. **Coil orientation is random.** Many mats have coils with mixed polarity, creating destructive fields and dead zones.
2. **Use aluminum or low-grade copper.** High-purity copper is essential for efficient conductivity.
3. **Inflate gauss numbers.** Add coil counts together or measure under unrealistic conditions.
4. **Don't produce true square waves.** Many produce distorted waves that reduce effectiveness.
5. **Use permanent magnets inside coils.** Some competitors embed neodymium magnets (3,000-5,000 gauss each) inside their coils. These create a static field that does nothing. They show the magnet sticking to the coil as "proof" of power, but it's misleading.
6. **Grounding sheets claim.** "The only thing grounding sheets do for you is they expose you to a really dirty PEMF because the ground in electrical socket is extremely dirty and there's a flow of electrons going through the wire. They don't ground you."
7. **Chinese factory mats.** Steeve went undercover in a Chinese PEMF mat factory and found "shocking truths about mass-produced products."

**His mat comparison calculator (2025):**
- Free on Patreon
- Input: coil diameter, number of coils, gauss reading, slew rate
- Calculates: total Weber, effective coverage area, constructive field rating
- Shows: "gauze is not everything. Weber is not everything. Slew rate is not everything, but they all harmonize and come together."

### 9. The Nextion Controller (His Premium Product)

**Source:** "PEMF world most advance controller 2025 Nextion user manual" (2025-09-25) + "The world's most advance PEMF program" (2024-04-13)

**Nextion features:**
- Touchscreen interface with programmable frequency sequences
- Quick start: Unipolar/Bipolar modes for Day (14Hz), Afternoon (8Hz), Sleep (1Hz)
- Session lengths: 20 min, 60 min, 4 hours, 8 hours
- **Frequency Train:** Custom programmable sequences where you set frequency, duration (in seconds), and duty cycle for each "wagon" (step)
- **Presets:**
  - Depression / winter blues: sweep from 1 to 85 Hz and back
  - Meditation: higher frequencies for alertness/focus
  - Studying / brain fitness
  - Anxiety and mood swing
  - Long COVID
  - Brain fitness: originally 1.5hr, reduced to under 1hr because people were exhausted
  - Brain defrag: for brain fog
- "Build Your Own" custom programs
- Duty cycle 1-20 (only affects heat, not healing)
- Screen dimming for sleep use
- Two simultaneous square waves (same frequency, different duty cycles)
- MOSFET-based for faster slew rate than ZK

**ZK-PP2K (basic controller):**
- Adjustable frequency and duty cycle
- Single square wave output
- More affordable entry point
- Same therapeutic effectiveness, just fewer programmable features

### 10. DIY Build Guide (Extracted from Transcripts)

**Source:** Parts 1-3 DIY series (2022-09) + "Step-by-step instruction" (2025-05-30) + "PEMF company do this wrong" (2024-09-06)

#### Parts List (Mini Mat - 6 Coils)

| Part | Specification | Notes |
|------|--------------|-------|
| Magnet wire | 22 gauge, enamel coated | Same wire used in motors/solenoids |
| Coil form | Propane bottle (~100mm diameter) | Standard green bottle |
| Tape | Electrical tape or Tuck tape | Tape as tight as possible |
| Controller | ZK-PP2K (without case) | Or Nextion for advanced |
| Power supply | 19.5V DC laptop-style | 6 amps max, 150 watts |
| Bathroom timer | Standard 120V timer | Proven, simple, safe |
| Wire (interconnect) | 16 gauge | For connecting coils |
| Solder + soldering iron | Standard electronics | For permanent connections |
| Heat shrink tubing | Various sizes | Protect connections |
| Compass | Standard magnetic compass | For polarity checking |
| Fan | 12V DC computer fan | For controller cooling |
| Buck converter | 24V to 12V | For fan power |
| Mat material | Foam or fabric base | Personal preference |
| Resistor | As specified per ZK docs | Current limiting |

#### Coil Construction Steps

1. **Wind 95 wraps** of 22-gauge magnet wire around propane bottle
2. **Tape immediately** - Put tape on as soon as you remove from bottle or it will "explode" (unwind)
3. **Tape four corners first** (90 degrees apart) to prevent bulging
4. **Wrap entire coil tightly** - "near the breaking point of the tape"
5. **Trim ends** and leave tails for interconnection
6. **Burn off enamel insulation** from wire ends with lighter/flame
7. **Sand the ends** with sandpaper until shiny copper visible
8. **Test resistance** - Each coil should be ~3.3 ohms

#### Mat Wiring

1. **Three coils in series** = one "series" (about 9.9 ohms)
2. **Two series wired in parallel** = one mini mat (6 coils total)
3. **Check polarity with compass** on EVERY coil before mounting
4. **All coils must face same direction** (north up on all)
5. **Solder all connections** permanently
6. **Heat shrink all solder joints**
7. **Test total resistance** before finalizing

#### Controller Wiring (Block Diagram)

```
120V AC → Bathroom Timer → Power Supply (120V to 24V DC)
                                    ↓
                    ┌───────────────┴──────────────┐
                    ↓                              ↓
            Buck Converter                    ZK-PP2K (PWM)
            (24V → 12V)                           ↓
                ↓                            Resistor
              Fan                                 ↓
                                          Connector Pin
                                                ↓
                                              MAT
```

#### For Bipolar (Advanced)

```
Power Supply → PWM (3 channel) → H-Bridge → Coils
                                     ↑
                              Direction channel
                              (half duty cycle of main PWM)
```

"If you don't want bipolar, the PWM that goes to Direction on the H-bridge just leave it at zero."

### 11. Specific Use Cases & Results

**From transcripts - these are Steeve's claims/experiences, not verified medical claims:**

| Condition | Approach | Steeve's Reported Result |
|-----------|----------|------------------------|
| **Bulging discs (neck)** | Regular PEMF mat use | Pain resolved; doctor said nothing could be done. "With PEMF that all went away." |
| **Ankle injuries** (army) | Regular mat use | Chronic pain improved |
| **Shoulder injury** (horse fall) | Custom shoulder brace with 4 PCB coils in parallel, battery powered | Claims "400% faster recovery." Custom device worn over sweater. |
| **Dementia (early onset)** | Custom pillow device ("T-PMF"), Nextion brain programs (frequencies sweeping 1-85Hz) | Claims stabilization and memory improvement. "Brain car wash" protocol. |
| **Back pain** | Full body mat | Two dedicated videos on back pain relief |
| **Eye health** | Custom small coil device | Reports improvement over time (2 update videos) |

| **Depression** | Frequency sweep program (1-85Hz and back) | Dedicated video on depression use case |
| **Long COVID** | Custom Nextion preset program | Preset built into Nextion controller |
| **Pet therapy** | Dog bed with PEMF coils, cat mat | Multiple videos on pet applications |
| **Psoriasis** | Regular mat use | Discussed in "reasons behind the design" |

### 12. Supplement Pairings

**Based on transcript search across all 54 transcripts:**

Steeve does NOT extensively discuss supplement pairings in his YouTube videos. His focus is purely on the engineering and physics of PEMF devices. The only nutritional references found:

1. **Vitamin D analogy:** "Your skin absorbs the UV that you don't see and you make vitamin D out of it. Your cells absorb the energy that the PEMF makes, they go to work, and then your body is allowed to do what it does best -- stay healthy, fix itself, prevent illnesses."

2. **Nutrient transport:** "PEMF therapy can be seen as a way to improve [the circulatory system] ... reducing inflammation opens up blood flow ... which allows nutrients and oxygen to reach cells."

3. **Cellular repair:** His framework is that PEMF optimizes the body's own systems, which would include nutrient absorption and utilization, but he doesn't prescribe specific supplements.

**However, Dr. Stephen Stokes (pain clinic interview featured on Steeve's channel, video 8dEOy5U9_XI) provides extensive supplement pairing guidance:**

4. **Binders (CRITICAL before PEMF sessions):** Dr. Stokes explains that PEMF therapy shakes toxins loose from sodium-potassium channels in cell membranes. These toxins enter the bloodstream and MUST be bound and eliminated. Recommended binders:
   - Activated charcoal
   - Apple pectin
   - Chlorophyll
   - Take binders BEFORE PEMF sessions

5. **Detox support shakes:** Dr. Stokes uses "SP Balance Detox" shakes - 1-3 per day during first 10 days of PEMF therapy to support detoxification pathways (liver, kidneys, bowels, sweat).

6. **Zinc supplementation:** Heavy metals deplete zinc universally regardless of metal type (arsenic, mercury, lead). Zinc is one of the primary minerals depleted, so supplementation is recommended.

7. **Hydration:** PEMF dehydrates cells (magnetic field pulses the cell membranes open). Minimum 64oz water/day, more during PEMF therapy. Include salt and sugar (electrolytes) - even Gatorade is acceptable during pulsing sessions.

8. **Mineral supplementation:** General mineral supplement recommended since heavy metals deplete multiple minerals.

**Dr. Stokes' warning:** "We see really sick people doing PMF therapy, high dosages of high-intensity PEMF therapy, and we see side effects: brain fog, anxiety, shortness of breath, ear ringing, headaches, nausea, rapid heartbeat, increased blood pressure. All things associated with poisoning." This is a Herxheimer-like reaction from toxin redistribution.

### 12b. Gauss Amplification Techniques

**Source:** "In an Attempt to Save the World" (F7dCrH55jGA) + "Got the vid, made mini PEMF mat" (TEsRpoJftuk)

**Metal backing plates dramatically increase gauss output:**

1. **Steel/ferromagnetic plates behind coils:** Steeve discovered placing steel plates behind coils creates a significant gauss amplifier. In his testing:
   - Baseline reading: ~27 gauss per coil
   - With steel plate behind coil: jumped to ~42-43 gauss (55%+ increase)
   - With maximum plate configuration: pushed close to 80+ gauss

2. **Stainless steel mesh (bug screen):** Ordered stainless steel mesh for screen doors as a lightweight alternative. Not all stainless steel is magnetic - depends on iron content and purity. If the mesh is ferromagnetic, it can serve as a cheap, lightweight gauss amplifier behind each coil.

3. **Coil stacking (poor man's thick wire):** Multiple thin-wire coils can be stacked on top of each other (same orientation) to effectively create a thicker wire coil. Three coils of 10 gauss each, properly stacked with matching polarity = 30 gauss combined. This is how you can use free salvaged wire from old motors/transformers to build effective coils at zero cost.

**Note:** Supplement pairing content may exist on his Patreon (paywalled) or in private consultations. The YouTube channel focuses on device engineering.

---

## Evolution of His Recommendations Over Time

### Phase 1: Early DIY (March-September 2022)
- Basic unipolar DC square wave focus
- Arduino-based controllers
- Simple builds, proof of concept
- Establishing that PEMF works and is buildable at home

### Phase 2: Bipolar Discovery (October 2022 - March 2023)
- Started pursuing NASA 10Hz bipolar square wave
- Failed with mechanical relays (couldn't do 10Hz)
- Successfully built electronic bipolar with H-bridge
- Pivoted from "unipolar is fine" to "bipolar may be better"
- Started Patreon community
- Still exploring optimal gauss ranges

### Phase 3: Design Philosophy Solidification (2023)
- Settled on 30-100 gauss sweet spot (tested 180 gauss, caused adverse effects)
- Developed Nextion touchscreen controller
- Built specialized devices: shoulder brace, pillow (T-PMF for dementia), pet beds
- Started exploring brain-specific frequencies for dementia
- Launched ZK product line

### Phase 4: Education & Debunking (2024)
- Major educational videos: Weber vs Gauss, waveform science, duty cycle
- Created mat comparison calculator
- More vocal about industry problems (wrong coil orientation, fake gauss claims)
- Released Nextion "Frequency Train" programmable sequences
- Started hiring help (VA Christine)

### Phase 5: Refinement & Commercial (2025)
- Launched PEMF Mat Source (US distribution through pemfmatsource.com)
- Released comprehensive mat comparison calculator (free on Patreon)
- Most technically detailed videos
- Nextion 2025 user manual (most advanced controller iteration)
- Focus shifting from pure DIY to also selling professional-grade mats
- ZK pricing: $1,000-$2,000 | Nextion pricing: $3,000+

### Key Position Changes:
- **Gauss:** Started without strong opinion, now firmly advocates 45-55 gauss sweet spot and anti-high-gauss
- **Bipolar vs Unipolar:** Started with unipolar only, now offers both but says "no research showing one is better"
- **Business model:** Started as pure DIY educator, now also sells commercial mats (but still teaches DIY)
- **Frequency programs:** Started with single fixed frequency, now offers complex programmable sequences for specific conditions
- **Coil count:** Early mats had fewer coils, now standardized at 15-20 for full body

## Safety Warnings (From Transcripts)

1. **Contraindicated for implanted medical devices** (pacemakers, etc.)
2. **Start slow - 20 minutes first** to allow body to detoxify
3. **Not a healing machine** - "PEMF does not directly heal you"
4. **24V maximum** - safe for skin contact
5. **If you don't know electrical, don't do it** - get help from someone experienced
6. **Different countries have different electrical standards** - be aware of your local voltage
7. **Low speed on drill when winding coils** - don't use high speed
8. **Don't exceed 100 gauss** - higher caused chest tightness in his testing
9. **Magnet wire is fragile** - "does not like to be bent, it'll break every single time"
10. **For educational and general wellness purposes only** - not medical advice

---

## Steeve's Background Story (From Interviews)

From the "I got interviewed" video (2024-11-07):
- Canadian, based in Edmonton, Alberta
- Background in heavy industrial electrical (power supplies, high voltage)
- Canadian Army veteran (resulting in chronic ankle injuries)
- Wife is a horse trainer specializing in gait training
- Had 3 bulging discs in neck - doctor said nothing could be done, just take Advil
- PEMF resolved his neck pain, which is what drove him to become an evangelist
- Originally was going to build something like an MRI-level device, then scaled down based on research
- "If I help five people build their own, I would have achieved my goal. I think we've surpassed that."
- Hired a VA (Christine, Philippines) for video editing and channel management
- Friend named Wilson (Patreon member) helped with Nextion programming - "The guy is borderline genius"

---

## Key Quotes

**On the industry:**
> "A lot of companies like to post all kinds of claims. We're going to see what we can do about demystifying some of that."

**On gauss marketing:**
> "When your coils are flat on the mat and you got 10 coils making 10 gauss each, you don't have 100 gauss. You still only have 10 gauss. But you're going to have a lot more Weber."

**On square wave:**
> "NASA concluded that the most effective waveform was, yeah no surprise there, the square wave."

**On duty cycle:**
> "The duty cycle does not improve or reduce the efficiencies of the PMF, the healing part. All it does is create heat on the mat."

**On high power:**
> "I built myself a mat that makes 180 gauss per coil. I got 10 coils on it and I don't like it. It gives me kind of a chest anxiety attack."

**On why he does this:**
> "Having witnessed the profound impact that this technology can have on people, whether it's a friend, family, a stranger... my mission is not merely a desire, it's like a need. I have to reach as many people as I can."

**On competitor coil tricks:**
> "The only way you can do this is if there are permanent neodymium magnets built into the triangle... which usually run between 3 and 5,000 gauss each of a STATIC magnetic field, and we all know that a static magnetic field does nothing."

---

## Summary: Steeve Bradet's PEMF Position in One Page

Steeve Bradet is a Canadian PEMF engineer who went from heavy-industrial electrical work to becoming one of the most technically rigorous voices in the DIY PEMF space. His core thesis is that the PEMF industry misleads consumers by focusing on gauss (magnetic pressure per flux line) when what actually matters is Weber (total magnetic flux coverage), waveform quality (square wave is proven superior), and constructive field design (all coils oriented same direction).

His recommended specs for therapeutic PEMF: 45-55 gauss per coil, square wave, 1-100 Hz frequency range, proper coil orientation verified with compass, 22-gauge magnet wire with 95 wraps on ~100mm form, powered by 19.5-24V DC supply through a PWM controller (ZK-PP2K basic or Nextion advanced).

He sells his own mats ($1,000-$3,000) through pemfmatsource.com but also freely teaches people how to build their own through his YouTube channel and Patreon community. His mat comparison calculator (free on Patreon) lets consumers objectively compare any PEMF mat by inputting coil specs.

His YouTube channel focuses primarily on engineering and physics, but the Dr. Stephen Stokes interview (featured on his channel) provides detailed supplement pairing guidance including binders (activated charcoal, apple pectin, chlorophyll), zinc, mineral supplementation, and aggressive hydration during PEMF therapy. See Section 12 above for details.

### Additional Side Effects Detail (from fxecu-l38Ok)

The side effects video features a pharmacist who uses PEMF in his practice. His perspective:

1. **TMJ/headaches:** Aggressive use on head/neck area can cause headaches. Not dangerous, but dose appropriately.
2. **Medication sensitivity:** PEMF improves cellular function, which can make medications MORE effective. Example: patient on hydrocodone became "loopier" because the medication was being absorbed better. Monitor medication doses.
3. **Bowel/bladder activation:** PEMF can activate sluggish bodily functions quickly. Patients sometimes need to interrupt sessions for the bathroom. This is a positive sign (system working), but be prepared.
4. **Dehydration:** Cells operating at higher levels = increased fluid needs. Always drink water and electrolytes post-session.
5. **Contraindications:** Any implanted electrical device (pacemaker, deep brain stimulator, vagal nerve system). These are absolute contraindications.

The pharmacist states: "As a pharmacist, with a completely straight face, I don't have a medication on the shelf that's as promising and as safe as PEMF therapy."
